electrochemical_impedance_test.label: Electrochemical impedance test
electrochemical_impedance_test.intent: Measure cell impedance under open-circuit conditions across SOC and life-stage checkpoints for qualitative or equivalent-circuit analysis.
applications: Ageing follow-up and ECM parameter fitting
required_equipment: Battery tester or potentiostat with integrated frequency response analyser

steps.name: Prepare full SOC start point
steps.details: Start from 100% SOC and rest for {rest_minutes} minutes before the first impedance spectrum.

steps.name: Acquire the impedance spectrum
steps.details: Measure a potentiostatic 5 mV rms spectrum from 10 kHz down to 10 mHz with 5-6 points per decade.

steps.name: Post-spectrum rest
steps.details: Rest for 10 minutes after each spectrum before changing SOC.

steps.name: SOC staircase
steps.details: Discharge at 1C to the next 20% SOC decrement and repeat the rest-spectrum-rest loop until 0% SOC, respecting the voltage limit and final CV completion logic at 0% SOC.

steps.name: Life-stage checkpoints
steps.details: Repeat the SOC-resolved EIS set at beginning, selected middle, and end-of-life checkpoints when the study needs SOH tracking.

data_acquisition: Keep cable, holder, and lead impedance controlled and documented because the chapter explicitly flags setup sensitivity.
Store the exact frequency vector so spectra remain comparable across checkpoints.

graphs: Resistance breakdown vs SOC and SOH
tables: Ra / Rb / Rt or equivalent-circuit fit summary

strict_reference_policy.summary: Keep the OCV condition, 20% SOC staircase, 5 mV rms signal, and 10 kHz to 10 mHz range locked. Only the life-stage checkpoint count should move after review.
tailorable_fields: which ageing checkpoints receive full EIS coverage

thermal_characterisation.label: Thermal characterisation
thermal_characterisation.intent: Determine the surface heat distribution that a cell develops during operation for later thermal-model and cooling-design work.
applications: Cooling-concept comparison at cell and module level

steps.name: Instrument the cell surface
steps.details: Place temperature sensors so the cell surface is covered without blind spots and keep lead losses observable where possible.

steps.name: Pseudo-adiabatic stage
steps.details: Insulate the cell as well as practical, then charge or discharge at defined currents while logging the surface heat distribution.

steps.name: Thermostated-plate repeat
steps.details: Remove the bottom insulation, attach the cell to a thermostated metal plate, and repeat the current profile at different cooling temperatures.

steps.name: Extended fixture variants
steps.details: If needed, extend the setup to multi-cell arrangements or busbar-cooling analogues once the single-cell stages are stable.

data_acquisition: Log all surface temperature channels and the exact current profile used in each stage.
Keep the insulation and cooling-fixture configuration versioned because it changes the heat-flow interpretation.

graphs: Temperature-gradient comparison between insulated and cooled stages

strict_reference_policy.summary: Keep the full-surface sensor coverage, pseudo-adiabatic first stage, and thermostated-plate repeat locked. Fixture extensions can be added only after review.

thermal_impedance_test.label: Thermal impedance test
thermal_impedance_test.intent: Identify thermal parameters from sinusoidal self-heating and surface-temperature response without destructive calorimetry.
required_equipment: Battery cell tester with sinusoidal-current capability

steps.name: Thermal stabilization
steps.details: Mount the cell in the minimal-contact fixture, place it in a 25 C chamber, and wait at least 3 h until cell and ambient temperatures match.

steps.name: Sinusoidal excitation
steps.details: Apply a sinusoidal current with alternating charge and discharge half-cycles every 3 s so SOC remains effectively constant while internal heat is modulated.

steps.name: Surface temperature recording
steps.details: Record the surface-temperature response with the IR thermopile and repeat the measurement over several frequencies between 0.1 mHz and 10 mHz.

steps.name: Thermal-parameter fit
steps.details: Fit a finite-difference thermal model by least squares until heat capacity, thermal conductivity, and convective exchange reproduce the measured spectrum.

data_acquisition: Store the excitation frequency, current amplitude, raw temperature trace, and filtered harmonic response for each run.
Keep the fixture contact area and optional thermal-camera homogeneity check documented.

graphs: Current, voltage, computed heat, and surface-temperature traces
tables: Fitted heat-capacity, conductivity, and convection coefficients

strict_reference_policy.summary: Keep the 25 C stabilization, sinusoidal self-heating, 0.1 to 10 mHz frequency range, and finite-difference parameter fit locked.
locked_planning_fields: 25 C chamber stabilization for at least 3 h
sinusoidal current excitation with 3 s half-cycles

quasi_static_thermal_tests.label: Quasi-static thermal tests
quasi_static_thermal_tests.intent: Quantify perpendicular swelling throughout SOC by synchronizing a dilatometer with a capacity-style test.
applications: Mechanical design support for cell or module fixtures
Supplementary SoC / SoH indicator development from swelling behaviour

steps.name: Mount synchronized dilatometer
steps.details: Mount a digital dilatometer and synchronize it with the battery tester before the electrical cycle begins.

steps.name: Run the capacity-style cycle
steps.details: Use the same electrical sequence as the capacity test while recording perpendicular swelling throughout the SOC range.

steps.name: Separate reversible and irreversible swelling
steps.details: Use the synchronized swelling trace to separate reversible SOC-linked behaviour from irreversible ageing-linked growth.

data_acquisition: Synchronize swelling, voltage, current, capacity, and temperature streams to the same timeline.
Retain the same electrical metadata as the paired capacity test so swelling results remain interpretable.

graphs: Swelling vs SOC during the reference cycle
tables: Peak swelling and residual swelling summary

strict_reference_policy.summary: Keep the synchronized dilatometer requirement and the capacity-test style electrical block locked. Interpretation depth can still move after review.
tailorable_fields: how deeply reversible and irreversible swelling are decomposed in reporting
